front: Film: Maria pawns her dowry bedsheets to get Antonio's bicycle out of the pawn shop for a job posting film posters (of Rita Hayworth); the bicycle is stolen the very next day
back: Bicycle Thieves / Ladri di biciclette (Vittorio De Sica, 1948)

front: Film: a restaurant scene cuts between poor Antonio and Bruno discussing death, and a wealthy family eating lavishly; ends with Antonio caught trying to steal an unattended bicycle, humiliated before Bruno
back: Bicycle Thieves / Ladri di biciclette (Vittorio De Sica, 1948)

front: Film: Antonio tails an old man to a church during a rainstorm; a suspect fakes a seizure; a fortune teller says he will find the bicycle 'quickly or not at all'; the bicycle is a 'Fides' brand
back: Bicycle Thieves / Ladri di biciclette (Vittorio De Sica, 1948)

front: Film: directed by Vittorio De Sica; screenplay by Cesare Zavattini; landmark of Italian neorealism; De Sica's other films include Sciuscia and Umberto D.
Film: a 1989 anti-commercialist parody by Maurizio Nichetti, in which commercial breaks and a power outage disrupt a director's reinterpretation
back: Bicycle Thieves (Vittorio De Sica) — parodied in Nichetti's film
